seo.title: Building AI Email Assistant with AG2 + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build ai email assistant with AG2 + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building an AI Email Assistant with AG2 enables sophisticated multi-agent workflows for intent detection, draft generation, attachment handling, and approval processes. However, the high-risk nature of email communication demands robust guardrails to prevent PII leaks, inappropriate tone, invalid recipients, and confidentiality breaches. Vex integration provides runtime verification to ensure safe, reliable email operations.

architectureOverview.heading: AI Email Assistant Architecture with AG2
architectureOverview.description:
The AI Email Assistant uses AG2's group chat capabilities with specialized agents: IntentDetector analyzes incoming emails, DraftGenerator creates responses, AttachmentHandler processes files, and Approver validates before sending. Agents collaborate in a moderated group chat to reach consensus on email actions. Vex hooks intercept messages for verification at each step.

Email flow begins with intent detection, followed by parallel draft and attachment processing, culminating in approval. This multi-agent design handles complex workflows but introduces risks like off-topic conversations or biased speaker selection.

AG2's enhanced group chat ensures orderly discussions while Vex enforces verification for PII filtering, tone checks, recipient validation, content accuracy, and confidentiality.

risks.name: PII Leakage in Drafts
risks.description: DraftGenerator agent inadvertently includes sensitive personal data from attachments or email bodies
risks.mitigation: Vex PII filtering hook scans all generated drafts before approval stage

risks.name: Inappropriate Tone Generation
risks.description: Agents produce unprofessional or offensive language due to conversation drift
risks.mitigation: Vex tone analysis verifies professionalism across all agent outputs

risks.name: Invalid Recipient Selection
risks.description: Group chat consensus selects incorrect or unauthorized email recipients
risks.mitigation: Vex recipient validation against approved contact lists

risks.name: Confidentiality Breach
risks.description: Agents reference or generate content containing proprietary information
risks.mitigation: Vex confidentiality enforcement blocks sensitive content transmission

implementation.title: Set Up AG2 Email Assistant Agents
implementation.description: Create specialized agents for the email workflow with Vex message hooks
implementation.code:
from ag2 import GroupChat, GroupChatManager

from vex import VexClient

vex = VexClient(api_key="your-vex-key")

intent_detector = Assistant(
    name="IntentDetector",
    llm_config={"model": "gpt-4"},
    message_hook=lambda msg: vex.verify("pii_filter", msg),
)

draft_generator = Assistant(
    name="DraftGenerator",
    llm_config={"model": "gpt-4"},
    message_hook=lambda msg: vex.verify("tone_check", msg),
)

approver = Assistant(
    name="Approver",
    llm_config={"model": "gpt-4o"},
    message_hook=lambda msg: vex.verify(["recipient_check", "confidentiality"], msg),
)

groupchat = GroupChat(
    agents=[intent_detector, draft_generator, approver],
    messages=[],
    max_round=10
)
manager = GroupChatManager(groupchat=groupchat, llm_config=llm_config)

implementation.title: Email Processing Workflow
implementation.description: Initiate group chat with incoming email for collaborative processing
implementation.code:
def process_email(incoming_email: dict):
    email_content = incoming_email['body']
    sender = incoming_email['from']
    recipients = incoming_email['to']
    
    # Start group chat with email context
    message = f"""
    New email received:
    From: {sender}
    To: {recipients}
    Subject: {incoming_email['subject']}
    Body: {email_content[:1000]}...
    
    Please analyze intent, generate appropriate response draft,
    handle any attachments, and prepare for approval.
    """
    
    result = manager.initiate_chat(
        None,
        message=message,
        max_turns=8
    )
    
    return {
        'draft': result.chat_history[-1]['content'],
        'approved': result.summary,
        'safe_to_send': vex.get_verification_status('final_approval')
    }

implementation.title: Configure Vex Verifications
implementation.description: Register specific verification policies for email assistant

implementation.title: Send Approved Email
implementation.description: Final transmission only after all Vex verifications pass
implementation.code:
import smtplib
from email.mime.text import MimeText

@manager.register_for_execution()
@manager.register_for_llm(name="email_sender")
def send_approved_email(draft: str, recipients: list, metadata: dict):
    # Final Vex verification
    final_check = vex_client.verify([
        "pii_filter",
        "tone_check",
        "recipient_check",
        "confidentiality"
    ], draft)
    
    if not all(final_check.results):
        raise ValueError(f"Email blocked: {final_check.failures}")
    
    # Send email
    msg = MimeText(draft)
    msg['Subject'] = metadata['subject']
    msg['From'] = metadata['from']
    msg['To'] = ', '.join(recipients)
    
    with smtplib.SMTP('smtp.company.com') as server:
        server.login(metadata['username'], metadata['password'])
        server.send_message(msg)
    
    return f"Email sent successfully to {len(recipients)} recipients"
